items.title: Are we in Claude Code peak hours right now?
items.text:
Your time Pacific Time

Peak hours: Weekdays 5 AM - 11 AM PT / 1 PM - 7 PM GMT | Full schedule | Advice

items.title: Advice for Working Around Peak Hours
items.text: Peak hours mean tighter session limits, not less total capacity. With a bit of planning, you can get the same amount of work done without hitting walls.

items.title: Plan your day around the schedule
items.text: Time window Status Best use Weekdays before 5 AM PT Off-peak Heavy refactors, batch operations Weekdays 5 AM - 11 AM PT Peak Light tasks, reviews, planning Weekdays after 11 AM PT Off-peak Resume intensive work Weekends (all day) Off-peak No restrictions

items.title: Shift heavy work to off-peak
items.text:
Token-intensive tasks burn through your session limits fast during peak. Move them to off-peak windows:

  • Large refactors spanning many files
  • Code generation for new features or boilerplate
  • Background agents running autonomously
  • Batch operations like bulk file processing or migrations

"If you run token-intensive background jobs, shifting them to off-peak hours will stretch your session limits further."

items.title: Reduce token usage during peak
items.text:
When you do work during peak hours, make every token count:

  • Keep prompts focused. Ask one thing at a time instead of multi-part requests.
  • Use /compact to compress conversation context when it grows large.
  • Disable unused plugins. Each active MCP server or skill adds to context size.
  • Don't dump entire files. Point Claude to specific lines or functions instead of pasting whole files.
  • Start fresh sessions. A clean conversation uses fewer tokens than continuing a long one.

items.title: Use peak hours for low-token tasks
items.text:
Some work barely touches your limits. Save these for peak hours:

  • Code review — reading diffs, spotting issues
  • Planning — designing architecture, writing specs
  • Quick questions — short Q&A about APIs or syntax
  • Documentation — writing or editing docs
  • Manual work — tasks you do yourself with Claude on standby

items.title: Spread work across tools
items.text:
Diversifying across AI tools means no single one bottlenecks you:

  • Google Gemini — strong for code and general tasks
  • OpenAI ChatGPT / Codex — alternative coding assistant
  • Local models (Ollama, LM Studio) — unlimited, no rate limits
  • GitHub Copilot — inline completions don't count against Claude limits

Use Claude for what it does best and fall back to alternatives when you're near your limit.

items.title: Weekly rhythm
items.text:
Your weekly token budget doesn't change — only how it's distributed. Think of it like electricity pricing: same total supply, cheaper at off-peak times.

  • Monday-Friday mornings (PT): conserve, do lightweight work
  • Afternoons and evenings: go heavy, no restrictions
  • Weekends: full capacity, great for deep work sessions

The weekly schedule page shows exactly where you are right now.

items.title: Peak hours by time zone
items.text:
Hours (Pacific) Hours (GMT/UTC) Hours (CET) Peak Weekdays 5 AM - 11 AM Weekdays 1 PM - 7 PM Weekdays 2 PM - 8 PM Off-peak All other times All other times All other times
  • Weekends are entirely off-peak
  • Weekly token limits stay the same — only the per-session distribution changes
  • ~7% of users hit session limits they wouldn't have before, particularly on Pro tiers

items.title: Daily breakdown
items.text:
Day 00-05 PT 05-11 PT 11-24 PT Monday Off-peak Peak Off-peak Tuesday Off-peak Peak Off-peak Wednesday Off-peak Peak Off-peak Thursday Off-peak Peak Off-peak Friday Off-peak Peak Off-peak Saturday Off-peak Off-peak Off-peak Sunday Off-peak Off-peak Off-peak

Total peak hours per week: 30 hours (out of 168)

See Practical Advice for tips on organizing your work around these hours.

items.title: About PeakClaude
items.text: PeakClaude is a simple real-time tracker that shows whether Claude Code is currently in peak or off-peak hours.

items.title: Why does this exist?
items.text:
In early 2025, Anthropic introduced peak hours for Claude Code to manage growing demand. During peak hours, 5-hour session limits are distributed more tightly, which can affect heavy users — especially on the Pro tier.

The schedule is straightforward but easy to forget, particularly across time zones. PeakClaude gives you a glanceable answer: peak or not?

items.title: How peak hours work
items.text:
Period When (Pacific Time) When (GMT/UTC) Peak Weekdays 5:00 AM - 11:00 AM Weekdays 1:00 PM - 7:00 PM Off-peak All other times All other times
  • Weekends are entirely off-peak.
  • Weekly token limits stay the same — only the per-session distribution changes.
  • About 7% of users are affected, mostly those running token-intensive workflows during peak windows.

items.title: Tips for working with peak hours
items.text:
• Schedule heavy jobs off-peak. Background tasks, large refactors, and batch operations are best run outside the peak window.
  • Use weekends. Saturday and Sunday have no peak restrictions at all.
  • Monitor your usage. If you're hitting session limits, check whether you're consistently working during peak hours.

items.title: How it works
items.text: PeakClaude runs entirely in the browser. It uses JavaScript's Intl.DateTimeFormat with the America/Los_Angeles timezone to determine the current Pacific Time, then compares it against the known peak schedule. No server, no API calls, no tracking.

items.title: Source
items.text: Peak hours were announced by Anthropic in December 2024: Update on session limits (r/ClaudeAI)

items.title: Built by
items.text: Created by pforret using MkDocs Material. Source code on GitHub.
